steps.title: Compare both proposed causes
steps.content: The goal is to compare the causes in two theories. Jacobs's theory says crown shyness happens when neighboring trees brush together and branches break; Ng's theory says branches stop growing when they detect shade from nearby branches.

steps.title: Reject one-sided summaries
steps.content: B says both scholars have theories but gives no causes. C names Ng's theory and calls Jacobs's an alternative, but it does not compare the causes. D explains only Jacobs's mutual abrasion theory and leaves out Ng's cause. The answer is A.
